titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Annie Finch Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 19, 2025 at 23:05
Updated by Tina Evans in December 2025 to describe four folders removed from printed material and returned to collection.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of writings, journals, correspondence and literary business files, teaching materials, personal and family papers, and computer and audiovisual media documenting the career and life of American author Annie Finch.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 94-97 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 98-100 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Annie Finch on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the Martin Greenblat Foundation Book Fund in American Literature, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Writings, 1965-2014. II. Literary Work and Correspondence, 1965-2015. III. Journals and Notebooks, 1966-2015, undated. IV. Teaching Materials, 1998-2014. V. Personal and Family Papers, 1848-2015. Series VI. Printed Material, 1842-2015. VII. Audiovisual and Computer Media, 1983-2003, undated.

bioghist.head: Annie Finch (1956-)
bioghist.p: Annie Finch is an American poet, translator, critic, performance artist, and educator. Her collections of poetry include Eve (1997) and Calendars (2003), which were finalists for the National Poetry Series, and Spells: New and Selected Poems (2012) . Finch's other works include the verse play Among the Goddesses: An Epic Libretto in Seven Dreams (2010) and the opera Marina (2003). She has written or edited a number of books on poetics and poetry, such as A Formal Feeling Comes: Poems in Form by Contemporary Women (1993), The Ghost of Meter: Culture and Prosody in American Free Verse (1994), Carolyn Kizer: Perspectives on Her Life and Work (2001), An Exaltation of Forms: Contemporary Poets Celebrate the Diversity of Their Art (2003), and The Body of Poetry: Essays on Women, Form, and the Poetic Self (2004) . Finch also edited the anthology Choice Words: Writers on Abortion (2020).
In 2013, Finch founded American Witch Enterprises, an online community and marketplace. In 2021, she founded Poetry Witch Press.

Finch graduated from Yale University with a B.A. in English in 1979, earned an M.A. in poetry and verse drama from the University of Houston in 1983, and completed a Ph.D. at Stanford University, where she studied versification, in 1990. She was the director of the Stonecoast MFA Creative Writing Program at the University of Southern Maine for ten years and has lectured on meter and poetry at institutions including the University of Toronto, University of California, Berkeley, and Harvard University.
Finch married Glen Brand in 1985, and they have two children, Althea Finch-Brand and Julian Finch-Brand.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Annie Finch Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.
Four items removed from printed material returned to the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
The collection consists of writings, journals, correspondence and literary business files, teaching materials, personal and family papers, and computer and audiovisual media documenting the career and life of American author Annie Finch. Included are drafts, ephemera, publishing correspondence, notes, and other materials relating to poetry collections and chapbooks by Finch, such as Among the Goddesses , The Encyclopedia of Scotland , Eve , Calendars , and Spells ; books on poetics, such as The Body of Poetry , The Ghost of Meter , and A Poet's Craft ; and edited or co-edited works, among them After New Formalism , An Exaltation of Forms , A Formal Feeling Comes , and Villanelles . Also included are Finch's writings on feminism and abortion; The Complete Poetry and Prose of Louise Labé: A Bilingual Edition , with poems translated by Finch; Marina , an opera libretto by Finch; and college and graduate school papers. The collection contains correspondence with colleagues, former teachers, editors, publishers, and friends, including Marie Borroff, Toi Derricotte, Jonathan Galassi, Marilyn Hacker, Langdon Hammer, Joy Harjo, John Hollander, Carolyn Kizer, Maxine Kumin, Diane Middlebrook, Molly Peacock, Adrienne Rich, Grace Schulman, and Ntozake Shange.
Teaching materials consist of notes, correspondence, syllabi, and readings for Finch's courses at the Stonecoast MFA program at the University of Southern Maine and Miami University of Ohio, as well as independent workshops.

Personal and family papers contain correspondence with Finch's parents, husband, children, and other relatives; artwork and realia; files pertaining to American Witch Enterprises, an online community and marketplace founded by Finch; and photographs and scrapbooks. Also included is research for an unpublished book by Finch on her great-aunt, educator and activist Jessie Wallace Hughan, and writings of her mother Margaret Rockwell Finch and father Henry Le Roy Finch.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Annie Finch Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven subseries: Poetry, Critical Work and Poetics, Edited Works, Music and Theater Projects, Translation, Early and School Writings, and Other Writings.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings can also be found in Series II. Literary Work and Correspondence, Series III. Journals and Notebooks, and Series V. Personal and Family Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consist of poetry, works on poetics, edited books, and other writings by Annie Finch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains individual and collected poems, including books such as Calendars , Eve , and Spells .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or folder title, with unidentified or unsorted materials filed last in the sequence.

title.part: Among the Goddesses: An Epic Libretto in Seven Dreams

did.unittitle: "Among the Epics: The Making of an Anti-Novel"
["Bad Mother Poems"] Final BMP's abortion poem (based on journal entry??)
Glen's comments on poems taken from Calendars
"Chain of Women" [crossed out title: "Three Generations of Secrets"]
An influence on E of S

E of S uncut [Salt Publishing version]
E of S cut [Salt Publishing version]
E of S reviews, PR [Salt Publishing version]
Advanced workshop [drafts of "Furious Sun in Her Mane" and "Three Generations of Secrets"]

title.part: The Furious Sun in Her Mane

scopecontent.p: Drafts, notebooks, and witchcraft-related materials formerly housed in Box 39.

scopecontent.p: Poem written for unveiling of Meredith Bergmann's September 11 sculpture at the Cathedral of St. John the Divine.

scopecontent.p: The published chapbook contains a selection from a longer "Lost Poems" manuscript.

Index of titles and first lines, corrected
"To the Unborn Child of Three Generations of Secrets"
Tom Allen redux (before the midterm election)
Yale ode [read at class of 1979 reunion]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of files relating to The Body of Poetry , The Ghost of Meter , and A Poet's Craft , as well as individual essays, notes, and research material on poetics.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or folder title, with unidentified or unsorted materials filed last in the sequence.

title.part: The Body of Poetry: Essays on Women, Form, and the Poetic Self

did.unittitle: "Dickinson and Patriarchal Meter: A Theory of Metrical Codes"
Celebrity poets [clippings, ephemera, photographs, workshop notes]
"Finding Form: How I Write Formal Poetry"

did.unittitle: Reviews, reader's reports, and other papers
Poetics list disc. of sonnet (not complete)
Poetry perceptions [printed material, reading list, outlines, notes]
Reference book articles on Annie Finch
Renaissance poetry + technology, by Ben Betram, notes on superb lecture
"The Sentimental Poetess in the World: Metaphor and Subjectivity in Lydia Sigourney's Nature Poetry"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials relating to A Formal Feeling Comes , An Exaltation of Forms , and other works edited or co-edited by Finch.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or folder title, with unidentified or unsorted materials filed last in the sequence.

did.unittitle: After New Formalism: Poets on Form, Narrative, and Tradition , reviews

title.part: Carolyn Kizer: Perspectives on Her Life and Work
An Exaltation of Forms: Contemporary Poets Celebrate the Diversity of Their Art

did.unittitle: [Publishing and author correspondence, book proposal, drafts]

title.part: A Formal Feeling Comes: Poems in Form by Contemporary Women

did.unittitle: Lofty Dogmas: Poets on Poetics , drafts
Lofty Dogmas: Poets on Poetics , flyers
Measure for Measure: An Anthology of Poetic Meters , drafts
Measure for Measure: An Anthology of Poetic Meters , proofs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes materials related to musical and theater collaborations by Finch, including Lily Among the Goddesses , Marina , and Wolf Song , in addition to files on ideas for plays, operas, and musicals, financial documents, correspondence, and printed material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or folder title.

did.unittitle: Landing Under Water: A Mermaid's Tale [puppet show]
"The Mermaid: A Verse Fable in Three Acts"
"The Mermaid Tragedy" and "Sheba in Eden," bound copy
Music - Sibyls Basset + research to file at home
"Our Mother God, You Make the Earth," talk at Union [?]
"Princess: An Opera for Children" [by Deborah Drattell, libretto by Finch]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials related to translations of poetry by Annie Finch, including the poems of Anna Akhmatova and Louise Labé.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or folder title.

did.unittitle: More Akhmatova translations - "Lot's Wife," "The White Bird"

title.part: The Complete Poetry and Prose of Louise Labé: A Bilingual Edition

did.unittitle: Lobe publicity and proofs, final stages

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists primarily of poetry and writings on poetics by Finch from the 1970s and 1980s. Included are notebooks and papers for college courses taught by Marie Borroff and at Yale, as well as very early poems by Finch.

did.unittitle: Oakwood School and Isaac E. Young Junior High School literary publications
School papers and tests, including early poems
"The Metrical Code & the Fate of Iambic Pentameter in American Poetry" [bound copy of submitted dissertation]
Proverbs [Kwesi Yankah, University of Ghana visiting scholar]
Fall 1978 John Hollander papers [course materials, notes and writings by Finch]
Literature class notes, papers, and handouts
Poem drafts, notes, letter, copy of The Yale Lit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of drafts and materials related to shorter works by Finch, a non-fiction book about spirituality, and notes on various topics, as well as some writings by other authors.

did.unittitle: "American Witch: A Guide to Reclaiming Your Spiritual Core"
Alix Baer senior paper typed by Finch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Baer designed the costumes and sets for an Encyclopedia of Scotland performance to music in 1982.

did.unittitle: From Fifty Farms, by Jared Hayes, proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a proof removed from printed material.

did.unittitle: "Homey's ILLiad: The Iliad as Gangster Rap" by Regie Gibson
Skandalon by T. R. Hummer , with comments by Finch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, contracts, printed material, and other papers relating to Annie Finch's career as an author and educator. Finch sometimes arranged incoming correspondence by first name, and letters from the same individual may be filed under either their first name or surname.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly by folder title or correspondent, with unsorted and unidentified materials filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence is also located in Series I. Writings, Series IV. Teaching Materials, and Series V. Personal and Family Papers.

did.unittitle: Association for the Study of Women and Mythology 2012 National Conference
"Compass of Poetics" agreement [with Dos Gatos Press]
Ephemera and memorabilia from readings and conferences
Performers + resources [clippings, flyers, playbills and programs]
Portland Press Herald '06 [clippings about Finch from multiple publications]
Shapes of Poetry in the 21st Century [conference]
West Chester [University, poetry conferences and seminars]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains items removed from printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consist of diaries, journals, and notebooks by Annie Finch relating to professional and personal matters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of files relating to courses taught by Annie Finch at Miami University of Ohio and University of Southern Maine's Stonecoast MFA in Creative Writing program, as well as materials documenting independent workshops and lectures, and job applications by Finch.

did.unittitle: "Constructing a Poem" talk at Colony Club
Exploring Form & Narrative [West Chester University poetry conference]
Poetry + architecture project [co-taught with Ben Jacks]
My office at Univ of Miami - 2003? [Memorabilia]
Quenton letter and draft, with AF edits
Stonecoast MFA, University of Southern Maine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes syllabi, teaching notes, student work and course evaluations, papers related to tenure, correspondence and materials relating to Stonecoast, and ephemera.

did.unittitle: WMR [Women, Mythology and Religion course proposal/description]
Misc. stuff re AF [class and workshop materials]
Application for faculty senate research funding
Stone House, email printouts regarding MFA program, notes, and ephemera
"Women, Language, and Literature," my course at Stanford
Yale course: A New Birth of Freedom: Antebellum Lit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence, artwork, photographs, memorabilia, writings, and printed material by or relating to Annie Finch, her parents Henry Le Roy Finch and Margaret Rockwell Finch, and other family.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Annie Finch, Henry Le Roy Finch, and Margaret Finch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains personal and family correspondence, files on American Witch Enterprises, and materials relating to spirituality, paganism, and tarot, as well as versions of Finch's unpublished memoirs and research she conducted for a biography of Jessie Wallace Hughan. Also included are a large amount of chronological "archive" files with materials concerning Finch's career as an author and educator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Annie / Daddy's file on me correspondence, clippings, other papers

Photographs of Camp Caribou, Maine, and rivers
19th century miniature Bibles and fortune cookie messages in wooden box
Personal papers [early artwork and school materials, correspondence, clippings]

Sigmund Freud letter to Stephen Zweig

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes hair clippings.

did.unittitle: Souvenirs of Annie's birth and early childhood
Stuff Roy gave me in Spring 2012 after cleaning out Maggie's house

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily materials related to Egypt, with ephemera in Arabic and a swatch of fabric.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook, ephemera, tablecloth with map of the London Underground.

did.unittitle: "The Rider Tarot Deck" booklet and eight Tarot cards
Travel, important trips to take - Maine & NE
2013 Italy + Prague trip with Glen, Althea memorabilia/ephemera
Unidentified children's book with AF illustrations?
Wills [Margaret Rockwell Finch and Henry LeRoy Finch]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains writings, notebooks, and correspondence of Henry Le Roy Finch. Included are submission letters to publishers and Finch's works on Israel, George Bush, Joseph Campbell, and Simone Weil.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Finch from siblings stationed overseas during World War II.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, family photographs, notes, printed material
Seekers Book at Buddhist Conference notebook
Souvenirs - Family / MR-Martha-Julie letters family clippings
Diary - HLF train trip to California when young
Correspondence from Annie Finch to parents
CPS mss, send copy to Swarthmore
Writings on Judaism, Christianity, and Israel
Outline for a theology of humanity
"The Two Zions" and other writings, correspondence
Misc. - Obsolete notes and corrected typescript of unidentified work on Wittgenstein
Alix Baer letters to Roy Finch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains poems, notebooks, correspondence, printed material, and other papers by or relating to Margaret Rockwell Finch.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Apollo 11 commemorative issue coin and stamp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of journals, magazines, newspapers, catalogs, programs, fliers, posters, and broadsides relating to Annie Finch and other authors, as well as Finch's family and her early life.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by alphabetically by folder title.

did.unittitle: Invocations to the Goddess...and then some: program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a program removed from printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of audio and video recordings and computer media documenting the life and career of Annie Finch.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Audiovisual Media and Computer Media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains audiocassette and videocassette tapes by or relating to Finch and her family.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by format, and alphabetically by title therein.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of floppy disks, zip disks, CDs, and DVDs relating to Finch and her father Henry Le Roy Finch.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
